turns.user_message: I noticed this morning that I completely skipped my usual playlist without even realizing it. It has only been a few days since I felt that familiar spark when I hear those tracks, but now it is just background noise. I tried picking up a new hobby last weekend and just stared at the supplies for an hour. It is strange how quickly things that used to feel exciting just feel completely flat. I keep waiting for that usual rush to come back, but it just is not there.

That same feeling has stuck around longer than I expected, turning into a weird daily routine. I catch myself going through the exact same motions every single afternoon, just watching the clock tick instead of actually enjoying anything. Even the little treats I used to look forward to just taste like cardboard now. I keep trying to force myself to care about the usual weekend plans, but my brain just refuses to cooperate. It is exhausting pretending everything is normal when the color has completely drained out of my week.

Now it feels like I am living inside a thick glass wall where nothing actually reaches me anymore. I will sit in the middle of a perfectly good afternoon and feel absolutely nothing but a heavy, hollow quiet. Every single thing I try to do just bounces off me, leaving me completely untouched by anything that happens around me. I keep checking my own reactions to see if I am broken, but the silence just keeps stretching out. I do not even know how to explain this blank space to myself when everything else keeps moving forward.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This just started recently.
